creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text:
Given that armv6 FreeBSD seems to require armv6+thumb2+vfp2 at a minimum, the compiler should also be configured to generate code for this architecture.

But this does not seem to be the case.  Instead, cc(1) seems to default to -march=armv6 (with hardfp I think) which is very restricting when compiling
thumb code.

Please change the way cc(1) is builz such that -march=armv6t2+fp is the default for armv6.
